title: pedicularis crenulata subsp. candida
intro: Pedicularis crenulata subsp. candida, also known as the white-lipped lousewort, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of neighboring plants, typically alpine plants. The medicinal properties of Pedicularis crenulata subsp. candida include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with this plant include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and acting as a diuretic, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
